check.action: Halt: "Worker returned an unexpected signal. Expected PHASE_COMPLETE or NEEDS_DECOMPOSITION; got: {{worker_return}}. Inspect worker output manually."

substep.title: Decomposition Handling (Lock 19)
substep.action:
Increment {{decomposition_strikes[current_slice.id]}} (default 0 → 1).
Per Lock 17.2 (three-strike abort): if the same slice has triggered NEEDS_DECOMPOSITION three consecutive times:

check.ask:
Slice {{current_slice.id}} has triggered NEEDS_DECOMPOSITION three consecutive times. Decomposition is not solving the size problem — the underlying approach likely needs rethink.
Recommendation: abort the workflow. The bundle remains as evidence. Restart planning session with the new understanding from these three decomposition attempts.
- Abort (Recommended) - Retry once more: I'll try one more decomposition + dispatch

check.action: Halt the skill. Codesmith primary surfaces abort message to user. Bundle on disk preserved as evidence.

substep.action:
Mini-replan per Lock 19: - Read worker's NEEDS_DECOMPOSITION return: - completed (what was done) - remaining (what's left) - suggested_sub_phases (if identifiable) - YOU own the plan's structural content. Edit {{plan_path}} to: - Mark slice {{current_slice.id}} as decomposed - Replace it with N sub-slices ({{current_slice.id}}.a, {{current_slice.id}}.b, ...) following the suggested sub-phases (or your own decomposition if suggestions are insufficient) - Re-order the slice queue: insert the sub-slices at the head, in dependency order
Per Lock 19: NEVER re-D. Design is locked. If decomposition reveals a design-level problem, that's the three-strike abort condition above firing.

uv run {base_dir}/scripts/stamp-frontmatter.py --file "{{plan_path}}" --note "Slice {{current_slice.id}} decomposed into sub-slices after NEEDS_DECOMPOSITION (strike {{decomposition_strikes[current_slice.id]}})"
Return to step 3 (dispatch worker for the new head of the slice queue).

substep.title: Capture PHASE_COMPLETE Outputs
substep.action:
Worker returned: - {{commit_sha}} - checks summary (tests/types/lint passes per slice's verification commands) - log subsection appended to plan doc
Re-read {{plan_path}}: confirm the slice's log subsection is present + the slice's checkboxes are marked. If either is missing, surface to user (worker discipline failure).
